findings.title: Runtime validator duplicated 5x instead of using Literal['AND','OR']
findings.suggested_fix: Declare all logic fields as Literal['AND', 'OR'] = 'AND' (from typing). Pydantic v2 enforces the constraint automatically, generates a correct JSON schema enum, enables static analysis, and eliminates all five validate_logic validator bodies. Same change applies in bot_manager.py, models.py, routes/backtest_quick.py (both classes), and routes/bots.py (Optional[Literal['AND','OR']] = None). The eval_rules() signature in signal_engine.py should also become logic: Literal['AND', 'OR'] for end-to-end static coverage.

findings.title: validate_logic copy-pasted identically across 5 model classes with no shared definition
findings.suggested_fix: The Literal approach (finding above) resolves this entirely. If runtime validation must be retained, extract a single module-level function _validate_logic(v: str) -> str and call it from each validator body, or define a reusable Annotated type alias once and reference it everywhere. Four identical 4-line blocks across 4 files is a maintenance hazard: any future extension requires 5 coordinated edits.

findings.title: cache_info() unpack is fixed but positional key indexing in _evict_expired() is still fragile
findings.suggested_fix: The 6-tuple unpack fix is correct for the immediate crash. However, _evict_expired() at line 410 still accesses the key via positional indices k[2] and k[3] (end and interval). If the tuple grows again those silently use the wrong elements. Use a star-unpack in cache_info: ticker, start, end, interval, source, *_ = key to tolerate future key growth. Longer-term, a NamedTuple for the cache key makes all sites self-documenting and eliminates fragile index arithmetic.

findings.title: UpdateBotRequest.validate_logic None guard is redundant and misleads readers
findings.suggested_fix: In Pydantic v2, field_validator in default (after) mode does not invoke the validator when the resolved value is None for Optional fields -- Pydantic short-circuits before the validator runs. The if v is not None guard is dead code. It is harmless correctness-wise but misleads readers. Verify: UpdateBotRequest(buy_logic=None) should parse cleanly; UpdateBotRequest(buy_logic="NAND") should raise ValidationError. The Literal approach eliminates this concern entirely.

residual_risks: eval_rules() in signal_engine.py accepts any str for logic and silently falls back to any(results) (OR semantics) for any value that is not literally 'AND'. A typo such as 'and' (lowercase) bypasses validation at the model layer and silently applies OR logic. Literal typing on the parameter would surface this at parse time.

The _fetch_cache key tuple structure is spelled out at three independent call sites (line 428 in _fetch, line 481 in async_ohlcv, and the unpack in cache_info). Future key changes require coordinated edits at all three. A NamedTuple for the key makes the structure single-source.
testing_gaps: No visible tests cover ValidationError paths for invalid logic values (e.g. 'and' lowercase, 'AND ' with trailing space, empty string). These are exactly the cases the new validators are designed to catch.
No test confirms UpdateBotRequest accepts None for all six logic fields without raising -- the None-handling correctness of bots.py line 79 is untested.
cache_info() has no smoke test after the 6-tuple fix. A test that populates _fetch_cache with a 6-tuple key and calls cache_info() would prevent regression if the key tuple changes again.
